name: United Kingdom
history: The Union Jack combines the crosses of St George (England), St Andrew (Scotland), and St Patrick (Ireland). The current design has been used since 1801 when Ireland joined the Union.
symbolism: Red cross of St George on white for England; white diagonal cross of St Andrew on blue for Scotland; red diagonal cross of St Patrick for Ireland.

name: France
history: The French tricolor emerged from the Revolution of 1789, combining the blue and red of Paris with the white of the Bourbon monarchy. Napoleon spread it across Europe.
symbolism: Blue stands for liberty, white for equality, and red for fraternity — the ideals of the French Revolution.

name: Germany
history: The black-red-gold tricolor originates from the uniforms of the Lützow Free Corps during the Napoleonic Wars (1813). It became the symbol of the liberal movement and German unity.
symbolism: Black represents determination, red represents bravery and strength, and gold represents generosity and the golden fields of Germany.

name: Russia
history: The white-blue-red tricolor was first used by Peter the Great in 1693, inspired by the Dutch flag. It was replaced by the Soviet red flag in 1917 and restored in 1991.
symbolism: White symbolizes nobility and openness, blue represents faithfulness and honesty, and red represents courage and love.

name: China
history: Adopted on September 27, 1949, the five-star red flag was designed by Zeng Liansong. The design was chosen from thousands of submissions for the new People's Republic.
symbolism: Red represents revolution and the Communist Party. The large star represents the Communist Party, while the four smaller stars represent the four social classes united under the party's leadership.

name: Japan
history: The Hinomaru (circle of the sun) has been used since at least the 7th century. It was officially adopted as the national flag in 1870 and confirmed by law in 1999.
symbolism: Japan is known as the 'Land of the Rising Sun'. The red disc represents the sun, which has been central to Japanese culture and mythology since ancient times.

name: Turkey
history: The star and crescent have been symbols of Istanbul since antiquity. The Ottoman Empire adopted this flag in 1844, and it was retained by the Turkish Republic founded in 1923.
symbolism: The crescent and star are ancient symbols of Islam and Turkish culture. The red field represents the blood of Turkish martyrs, and the white represents peace.

name: Uzbekistan
history: The flag was adopted upon independence from the Soviet Union in 1991. The crescent and twelve stars reflect the nation's Islamic heritage and twelve calendar months.
symbolism: Blue represents the sky and water, white represents peace, and green represents nature. The crescent symbolizes the new state, and the twelve stars represent the months of the Islamic calendar.

name: Kazakhstan
history: Adopted on June 4, 1992, the flag features a light blue field symbolizing the endless sky and a golden sun with 32 rays representing abundance and prosperity.
symbolism: The light blue represents the sky, freedom, and cultural unity of Kazakh people. The golden sun symbolizes wealth and abundance. The soaring eagle represents freedom and the flight of the Kazakh spirit.

name: India
history: The Indian tricolor was adopted on July 22, 1947, shortly before independence. It evolved from the flag of the Indian National Congress, replacing the spinning wheel with the Ashoka Chakra.
symbolism: Saffron represents courage and sacrifice, white represents peace and truth, and green represents faith and fertility. The Ashoka Chakra (24-spoke wheel) represents the eternal wheel of law.

name: Brazil
history: The current flag was adopted on November 19, 1889, shortly after the proclamation of the republic. The green and yellow come from the imperial flag.
symbolism: Green represents the forests and nature, yellow represents the gold of Brazil, blue represents the sky over Rio de Janeiro on November 15, 1889. The 27 stars represent Brazil's states and federal district.

name: Canada
history: The maple leaf flag, known as l'Unifolié, was adopted on February 15, 1965 after a lengthy debate. It replaced the Canadian Red Ensign, ending the colonial symbolism.
symbolism: Red and white are Canada's official colors since 1921. The maple leaf has been a symbol of Canada since the 18th century and represents the country's natural beauty.

name: Australia
history: The current flag design was selected in a competition in 1901 and has been used since Federation. It combines the Union Jack, the Commonwealth Star, and the Southern Cross.
symbolism: The Union Jack represents Australia's British heritage. The seven-pointed Commonwealth Star symbolizes the federation of states and territories. The Southern Cross is a prominent constellation visible from Australia.

name: Italy
history: The Italian tricolor was inspired by the French tricolor and first used in 1797 in the Cispadane Republic. It became the national flag of unified Italy in 1861.
symbolism: Green represents the hills and plains, white represents the Alps and snow-capped mountains, and red represents the blood spilled for Italian independence.

name: Spain
history: The red and yellow colors have been used since the 18th century under King Charles III. The current flag was formally adopted in the 1978 constitution and confirmed in 1981.
symbolism: The colors red and yellow (gold) represent the traditional colors of the Spanish kingdoms. The coat of arms features the Castle of Castile, the Lion of León, the Chains of Navarre, the Bars of Aragon, and the Pomegranate of Granada.

name: Ukraine
history: The blue and yellow flag dates back to medieval Galicia. It was used briefly in 1918 and restored after independence in 1991. The colors appear in the Galician-Volhynian coat of arms.
symbolism: Blue represents the sky over Ukraine, and yellow represents wheat fields — a symbol of Ukraine's agricultural heritage and the golden fields of the Ukrainian steppe.

name: United Arab Emirates
history: The UAE flag was designed by Abdullah Mohamed Al Maainah and adopted on December 2, 1971, the day of UAE independence and federation of the seven emirates.
symbolism: Green represents fertility and the nation's prosperity, white represents peace, black represents the oil wealth, and red represents strength and the courage of warriors.

name: Saudi Arabia
history: The green flag with the Shahada and sword has been used since the founding of the modern Saudi state. Green is the color of Islam and the prophet Muhammad.
symbolism: Green represents Islam. The Shahada (Islamic declaration of faith) and sword represent the Islamic character of the state. The sword represents justice administered by the House of Saud.

name: South Korea
history: The Taegukgi was first used in 1883 during the Joseon Dynasty. The current design was adopted in 1950, though it has undergone minor modifications.
symbolism: White background represents peace and purity. The Taeguk (yin-yang) represents balance of opposing forces. The four trigrams represent heaven, earth, water, and fire from the I Ching.
